title: Get Soft Feet with 8 Effective Herbal Creams for Cracked Heels

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for cracked heels because they help to soften and moisturize dry skin, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of cracks.

Aloin is good for cracked heels because it has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to heal and protect the damaged skin, reducing inflammation and preventing infections that can make cracked heels worse.
Vitamins A C E help cracked heels by promoting new skin growth, reducing inflammation, and improving collagen production to make the skin smooth and healthy again.
Polysaccharides are good for cracked heels because they help to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin, promoting healthy healing and softening of the cracked skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is a great treatment for cracked heels because it has many helpful properties. The cream is made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, which is rich in vitamins A, C, and E. These vitamins help to heal and protect the skin. Aloe vera also has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the heels. Additionally, the cream moisturizes the skin, keeping it soft and hydrated. This helps to lock in moisture and reduce the dryness that often causes cracked heels. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the skin makes it a gentle treatment that won't irritate the skin further.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream to help with cracked heels, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you will need to make a herbal infused oil by combining 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus dried leaves with 2 cups of a neutral oil, such as sweet almond oil, in a clean glass jar. Next, place the jar in a saucepan of simmering water and let it infuse for 2-3 hours, or overnight. After infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the leaves. Then, mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of a moisturizing base, such as coconut oil or shea butter, and add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil. Finally, melt the beeswax in a double boiler and slowly add the infused oil mixture, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens and cools to form a smooth, creamy texture. Your Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream is now ready to use, apply it to your cracked heels to help soften and heal the skin.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for cracked heels

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin helps to soften and heal cracked heels by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the affected area, making it easier to recover from dry and damaged skin.
Saponins are good for cracked heels because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soften and heal dry, cracked skin.
Tannins are good for cracked heels because they help to heal and protect the skin by reducing inflammation and promoting the formation of a new layer of skin cells.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is good for cracked heels because it helps to heal and protect the skin. The cream contains special oils that reduce inflammation and fight off germs that can make the cracks worse. It also has antioxidant properties that keep the skin healthy and strong. When applied to cracked heels, the cream creates a protective barrier that locks in moisture and keeps it from getting dry and cracked again. This helps to soften and smooth out the skin, making it less painful and less noticeable. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce redness and swelling, making it a great treatment for cracked heels.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream for cracked heels, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, combine 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Heat the mixture until the beeswax has melted and the coconut oil and shea butter are smooth and well combined. Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool slightly before adding 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus oil (Eucalyptus globulus) and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil. Stir well to mix the oils with the cream base. Next, pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and thicken at room temperature. Once the cream has hardened, it's ready to use. To apply, gently massage a small amount onto your cracked heels, paying special attention to any dry or cracked areas. Eucalyptus globulus oil has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and heal dry, cracked skin.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for cracked heels

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Catechins help cracked heels by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth, which can help to repair and soften dry, cracked skin.
Tannins are good for cracked heels because they help to dry out the wet areas of the skin, which in turn helps to prevent bacterial growth and reduces the risk of infection.
Quercetin is good for cracked heels because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and repair dry, cracked skin by reducing redness and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
Terpinen-4-ol is good for cracked heels because it has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to heal and protect the skin on the heels from infections and further damage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream is a great solution for cracked heels because it's made from natural ingredients. The tea tree oil in this cream helps to heal and soothe dry skin, reducing inflammation and redness. The cream also contains moisturizing properties that lock in moisture, keeping your skin hydrated and soft. As a result, the cracked skin begins to repair itself, and new skin starts to grow. The cream also prevents further drying out of the skin, which helps to prevent new cracks from forming. When used regularly, Melaleuca alternifolia cream can help to soften and smooth out cracked heels, making them look and feel healthier.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream for cracked heels, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need eucalyptus globulus essential oil, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Begin by melting the coconut oil and beeswax in the double boiler, stirring occasionally until they are fully incorporated. Next, add the shea butter and let it melt, stirring constantly to prevent burning. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add the eucalyptus globulus essential oil, which contains antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and moisturize dry skin, and vitamin E oil, which provides antioxidant protection. Stir well to combine and let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a container. Once the cream has cooled and set, it is ready to use, applying it generously to cracked heels and massaging it in to help soften and repair dry skin.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for cracked heels

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for cracked heels because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal dry, cracked skin, reducing pain and discomfort.
Flavonoids are good for cracked heels because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and heal dry, irritated skin, reducing pain and discomfort.
Polyphenols are good for cracked heels because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and heal dry, damaged skin, reducing pain and promoting healthy skin cell growth.

Tannins are good for cracked heels because they have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to prevent the growth of fungi and bacteria that can cause infections and make heel cracks worse.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a natural remedy that can help to heal and soften cracked heels. This is because Glycyrrhiza glabra has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling in the cracked heels. The cream also contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to lock in moisture and protect the skin from dryness. This makes it easier for the skin to repair itself and reduce the appearance of cracks. The cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for people with sensitive skin. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra cream can help to strengthen the skin's barrier function, making it less prone to dryness and cracking. The cream can also help to soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from the discomfort and pain associated with cracked heels.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream to help with cracked heels, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need to dry Eucalyptus globulus leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them for at least 24 hours. Once the leaves are dry, you'll need to make a herbal infusion by steeping the dried leaves in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a saucepan over low heat for about 2-3 hours. After the infusion is complete, you'll strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the Eucalyptus leaves. Next, you'll mix the Eucalyptus-infused oil with a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the mixture has melted and combined, you'll pour it into small containers and let it cool until it solidifies into a creamy paste. This Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream can then be applied to cracked heels to help soothe and moisturize dry skin.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for cracked heels

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for cracked heels because they have moisturizing and antifungal properties that help to soften and protect dry, damaged skin.

Tannins are good for cracked heels because they have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and heal dry, cracked skin, reducing pain and promoting healthy skin regeneration.

Flavonoids are good for cracked heels because they help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin regeneration, making them an effective natural remedy for soothing and healing dry, cracked skin on the heels.
Polyphenols are good for cracked heels because they help to heal and protect the skin by reducing inflammation and promoting the growth of new skin cells.
Allantoin is good for cracked heels because it helps to soften and moisturize dry skin, promoting healing and reducing the appearance of cracks and fissures.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale cream is a great way to treat cracked heels. This cream is made from comfrey, a plant that has been used for centuries to heal wounds and dry skin. Comfrey has special properties that help to speed up the healing process and make skin stronger. When you apply the cream to your cracked heels, it penetrates deep into the skin and starts to repair the damaged areas. The cream also helps to lock in moisture, keeping your skin hydrated and soft. As a result, the cracks in your heels start to heal, and your skin becomes smoother and healthier. The cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it suitable for people with sensitive skin. With regular use, you can expect to see noticeable improvements in the appearance and feel of your cracked heels.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream for cracked heels, start by infusing dried eucalyptus leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Fill a clean glass jar with the dried eucalyptus leaves, leaving about an inch of space at the top, and then pour the carrier oil over them. Let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to allow the eucalyptus' healing properties to infuse into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the eucalyptus leaves. Next, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a rich, spreadable cream. Melt the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler or in the microwave, then slowly add it to the infused oil while constantly stirring. Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and cools, then transfer it to a clean container and store it in the fridge to slow down the oxidation process. Finally, apply the cream to your cracked heels after a warm bath or shower, massaging it gently into the skin to lock in moisture and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Symphytum officinale herbal creams for cracked heels

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for cracked heels because they help to soften and moisturize dry skin, reduce inflammation, and promote healing of cracks and fissures.

Polysaccharides are good for cracked heels because they have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that help to soothe and soften dry, cracked skin on the heels, reducing pain and promoting healthy skin regeneration.
Glucomannans are good for cracked heels because they help to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing inflammation in the cracked skin.
Vitamins A are good for cracked heels because they help to lock in moisture, reduce inflammation, and promote healing of the skin to repair dry and cracked areas.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera cream is a great solution for cracked heels because it helps to heal and moisturize the dry skin. The gel inside the aloe vera plant has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the cracked heels. When you apply aloe vera cream, it creates a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and preventing further dryness. This helps to repair the cracks and soften the skin, making it less painful to walk or stand. Aloe vera cream also has antibacterial properties that prevent infections in the cracked heels, which is especially important if the cracks are deep or bleeding. As you continue to use aloe vera cream, the skin on your heels becomes healthier and smoother, reducing the appearance of cracks and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream for cracked heels, start by infusing dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a saucepan over low heat for about 2 hours or by letting it steep in the oil for 24 hours in the refrigerator. Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, and discard the solids. Next, melt 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals of 10-15 seconds. Once melted, let it cool slightly before mixing it with the infused oil, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 teaspoons of honey. Whip the mixture using a hand mixer until it becomes smooth and creamy, and pour it into a clean jar. The Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream is now ready to use, and its antiseptic and moisturizing properties will help to soothe and heal cracked heels.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for cracked heels

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Roserinic acid is good for cracked heels because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling, promote healing, and soften dry skin.
Menthol is good for cracked heels because it helps to cool and soothe the skin, reducing inflammation and pain, and promoting healing and moisturizing to dry, cracked skin.

Polyphenols are good for cracked heels because they have antibacterial and moisturizing properties that help to soothe, soften, and protect dry skin, promoting healing and preventing further cracking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint oil, is a popular ingredient in creams for cracked heels. The reason is that peppermint oil has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to fight off infections and reduce pain and swelling in the skin. When applied to cracked heels, peppermint oil cream can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort. The menthol in peppermint oil also helps to improve blood circulation to the area, which can aid in the healing process and reduce the appearance of cracks. Additionally, peppermint oil cream can help to lock in moisture, keeping the skin hydrated and soft. This can be especially helpful for people with dry skin, as it can help to prevent further cracking and irritation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams that help with cracked heels, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. Start by infusing eucalyptus leaves in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for a few weeks to extract the beneficial properties. To do this, place a handful of eucalyptus leaves in a clean glass jar and pour the carrier oil over them, making sure the leaves are completely covered. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and shake it every day for 2-3 weeks. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a cream base, such as shea butter or cocoa butter, in a ratio of about 1:1. You can also add some beeswax to thicken the cream and make it more stable. Finally, heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is melted and the cream is smooth and even. Pour the cream into clean jars and let it cool before using it to soothe and moisturize cracked heels.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for cracked heels

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Tannins are good for cracked heels because they have antifungal properties that help prevent infections and promote healing of dry, cracked skin.
Quercetin in Eucalyptus Globulus is good for cracked heels because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe, soften, and protect the dry and damaged skin on the heels.
Catechins are good for cracked heels because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and heal dry, irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort.
Polyphenols help to repair and soften cracked heels by reducing inflammation and promoting new skin cell growth, which leads to faster healing and healthier skin.
Cineole is good for cracked heels because its moisturizing and antibacterial properties help to soften dry skin, reduce inflammation, and prevent infection, promoting healthy and smooth skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is a great treatment for cracked heels. The main reason is that it has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. When you apply the cream to your cracked heels, it cools and soothes the skin, making it feel better. The cream also moisturizes the skin, helping to repair and soften it. Eucalyptus globulus has antimicrobial properties, which prevent infection and promote healing. As a result, the cream helps to reduce the appearance of cracked heels and makes them feel smoother and more comfortable. Using eucalyptus globulus cream regularly can also help to prevent cracked heels from happening in the first place, by keeping the skin hydrated and healthy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Eucalyptus globulus herbal cream for cracked heels, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by infusing dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Simply pour 1 part of dried Eucalyptus leaves into 3 parts of carrier oil and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter to create a smooth, creamy texture. Melt the beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler or in the microwave, then slowly pour it into the infused oil, stirring constantly until it thickens. Finally, add a few drops of vitamin E oil to help preserve the cream and prevent spoilage. Transfer the cream to a clean glass jar and let it cool before applying it to your cracked heels, ideally after a warm bath or shower when your skin is soft and ready to absorb the moisturizing properties of the Eucalyptus globulus cream.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for cracked heels

intro_desc: Cracked heels can be painful and unsightly. If left untreated, they can lead to infection and make walking uncomfortable. This is why it's essential to fix the issue. Using herbal creams is a good way to treat cracked heels. These creams often contain Aloe barbadensis, a plant known for its soothing and moisturizing properties. It helps to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and cracking. Aloe barbadensis also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling. Another common herb used in these creams is Calendula officinalis, which has antiseptic properties that help to prevent infection. It also promotes wound healing and tissue repair, making it an effective ingredient in treating cracked heels. By using herbal creams containing Aloe barbadensis and Calendula officinalis, you can help to heal and prevent cracked heels, improving the overall comfort and appearance of your feet. This can boost your confidence and make it easier to engage in physical activities, improving your quality of life.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal creams to remedy cracked heels is a blend of 'Aloe barbadensis', 'Calendula officinalis', and 'Melaleuca alternifolia'. 'Aloe barbadensis' is rich in vitamins and minerals that help to soften and moisturize the skin, reducing dryness and cracking. 'Calendula officinalis' has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting healing and reducing redness. 'Melaleuca alternifolia' has antibacterial properties that prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for skin repair. This combination works well together because 'Aloe barbadensis' provides the initial hydration, 'Calendula officinalis' helps to calm and soothe the skin, and 'Melaleuca alternifolia' protects the skin from further irritation.
supplementary_avoid: When choosing herbal creams for cracked heels, it's essential to be cautious of certain ingredients that may not be suitable. One herb to avoid is 'Capsicum annuum', also known as cayenne pepper. While it has anti-inflammatory properties, its high acidity can irritate skin and make cracked heels worse. Another herb to be careful with is 'Thuja occidentalis', also known as eastern white cedar. This herb can be toxic if ingested, and applying it to cracked heels can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. 'Cassia alata', also known as sirenja, contains a compound that can cause skin redness and irritation if used on cracked heels. 'Equisetum arvense', also known as horsetail, can be too harsh for dry skin and may make cracked heels worse. Lastly, 'Rhus aromatica', also known as fragrant sumac, can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions due to its high concentration of essential oils. These herbs can be too harsh or irritating for cracked heels, so it's best to choose creams with gentler ingredients.
supplementary_other_remedies: Exfoliating with sugar or salt scrubs can help with cracked heels. These natural abrasives gently remove dead skin cells, revealing smoother skin underneath. The friction from the scrub helps to break down thickened skin, promoting healing and softening. By applying a sugar or salt scrub to dry skin, usually 1-2 times a week, you can help to gradually reduce the appearance of cracks.  Moisturizing with natural oils, such as coconut or olive oil, can also be beneficial. These oils contain fatty acids that help to lock in moisture, reducing dryness and cracking. Applying a thick layer of oil to the heels and massaging it in can help to soften the skin and reduce the appearance of cracks. The oils can also help to protect the skin from further dryness, making them a useful addition to your cracked heel treatment.  Another natural remedy for cracked heels is soaking the feet in warm water. This helps to soften the skin, making it easier to remove dead skin cells and reducing the appearance of cracks. By adding Epsom salt or baking soda to the water, you can also help to reduce inflammation and soften the skin further. The warmth of the water can also help to increase blood flow to the area, promoting healing and reducing dryness.  Using a pumice stone or foot file can also help with cracked heels. These tools gently remove dead skin cells, revealing smoother skin underneath. The friction from the stone or file helps to break down thickened skin, promoting healing and softening. By using a pumice stone or foot file regularly, usually 1-2 times a week, you can help to gradually reduce the appearance of cracks.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with cracked heels often experience other skin problems due to dryness and moisture loss. These issues can be relieved with medicinal herbs. For instance, eczema is a common skin condition where the skin becomes dry, itchy, and inflamed. Aloe vera, rich in vitamins A, C, and E, can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting hydration.  Athlete's foot is another issue that can arise due to cracked heels. This fungal infection causes itching, burning, and cracked skin on the feet. Tea tree oil, known for its antifungal properties, can help combat fungal growth and reduce symptoms.  Dry and cracked skin on the heels can also lead to fungal infections like ringworm. Licorice root, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties, can help combat fungal growth and promote skin healing.  Additionally, people with cracked heels may experience calluses and hard skin patches on their feet. Turmeric, with its anti-inflammatory properties, can help reduce inflammation and promote skin healing, making it easier to remove calluses.  It's worth noting that while these herbs can provide relief, it's essential to maintain good foot hygiene, wear breathable shoes, and stay hydrated to prevent future occurrences of cracked heels and related skin issues.
